description.description: High-level summary of the component's role and purpose.

is_data_store.description: True if this component primarily represents a shared data store (e.g., a database, a cache, a state repository).

boundary_definition.description: Defines what is inside versus outside the component, clarifying its scope and dependencies.

summary.description: A narrative description of the component's boundary.

external_dependencies.description: A list of external systems or components this LCOMP depends on that are not described elsewhere as LCOMPs.

allocated_functions.description: The set of behaviors and logic the component is responsible for (what it does internally).

traces_to.description: The specific requirements this function satisfies.

logical_ports.description: Logical interaction points for communication with the outside world.

nature.description: The technological nature of the communication channel.

interfaces.description: The contracts defining what is exchanged across logical ports.

port_id.description: The logical port this interface is exposed on.

input.description: The list of input parameters for the interface.

output.description: The list of output attributes from the interface.

subcomponents.description: [RARE] A breakdown of internal complexity. Only for highly complex components.

functional_exchanges.description: Describes how functions communicate across component boundaries.

interface_id.description: The interface used for this exchange.

rationale_and_constraints.description: Explanation for the component's existence and its non-functional constraints.

rationale.description: Why this component exists. e.g., 'To isolate safety-critical logic.'

constraints.description: Logical properties and constraints, linking to NFRs.
